events.text: Patient is a 68-year-old Caucasian male, height 5 feet 11 inches, weight 210 pounds, ECOG performance status 1.

At age 68, patient presented with 2-month history of rib pain and exertional dyspnea; workup revealed hemoglobin 9.0 g/dL, creatinine 1.3 mg/dL, calcium 10.6 mg/dL, beta-2 microglobulin 5.2 mg/L, albumin 3.1 g/dL consistent with ISS Stage III multiple myeloma.

At age 68, bone marrow biopsy from site C42.1 showed 55% plasma cells with histology consistent with multiple myeloma (ICD-O morphology 9732/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CD138, CD38, and cytoplasmic IgG kappa; CD56 negative which is atypical; flow cytometry confirmed aberrant plasma cells comprising 48% of total cellularity.

At age 68, FISH analysis on bone marrow aspirate showed t(4;14) in 55% of plasma cells; no del(17p) or t(14;16); gain(1q21) detected in 30% of cells; R-ISS Stage III based on ISS III, LDH elevated at 265 U/L, and high-risk t(4;14).

At age 68, serum protein electrophoresis showed M-spike of 5.1 g/dL in the gamma region; immunofixation confirmed IgG kappa monoclonal protein; serum free light chain kappa 310 mg/L, lambda 15 mg/L, kappa/lambda ratio 20.7; marked rouleaux formation on peripheral blood smear; serum viscosity mildly elevated at 2.8 centipoise.

At age 68, PET/CT showed widespread FDG-avid skeletal disease involving the axial and appendicular skeleton with at least 15 focal lesions; largest in the right 8th rib measuring 3.8 cm with cortical destruction; FDG-avid lesion in the sacrum with SUV 9.2; no extramedullary plasmacytomas.

At age 68, MRI of the pelvis and femora showed diffuse marrow replacement with multiple focal lesions in the sacrum and bilateral iliac bones; a 2.5 cm lesion in the left femoral neck raised concern for pathologic fracture risk and orthopedic consultation was recommended.

At age 68, patient received prophylactic radiation therapy to the left femoral neck lesion, 20 Gy in 5 fractions, to reduce fracture risk; concurrent initiation of denosumab 120 mg subcutaneously monthly given creatinine clearance of 52 mL/min.

At age 68, initiated VRd induction therapy with standard dosing on 28-day cycles; plan for 4 to 6 cycles followed by consideration of autologous stem cell transplant; started acyclovir prophylaxis and low-molecular-weight heparin for DVT prevention given higher thrombotic risk.
At age 68, cycle 1 of VRd completed; SPEP at end of cycle 1 showed M-spike decreased to 3.8 g/dL indicating early response.
At age 68, cycle 2 of VRd completed; M-spike further decreased to 2.2 g/dL; serum free kappa light chain decreased to 125 mg/L; hemoglobin improved to 10.1 g/dL.

At age 68, after cycle 3, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy in bilateral lower extremities and grade 2 constipation; bortezomib dose reduced to 1.0 mg/m2; aggressive bowel regimen instituted.

At age 68, cycles 4 through 6 of VRd completed with dose-reduced bortezomib; post-induction SPEP showed M-spike 0.5 g/dL; serum free kappa light chain 28 mg/L; response classified as very good partial response.

At age 69, pre-transplant evaluation confirmed fitness for autologous stem cell transplant despite age 69; cardiac evaluation showed EF 58% with no significant valvular disease; pulmonary function adequate.
At age 69, received high-dose melphalan 200 mg/m2 followed by autologous stem cell transplant; cell dose 4.2 million CD34-positive cells per kilogram.

At age 69, post-transplant complicated by grade 3 mucositis, grade 2 diarrhea, and febrile neutropenia on day plus 8; blood cultures grew coagulase-negative staphylococcus treated with vancomycin; engraftment on day plus 15.

At age 69, day plus 100 bone marrow biopsy showed 4% plasma cells; SPEP no M-spike; immunofixation showed faint IgG kappa band; MRD by multiparameter flow cytometry at 10^-5 sensitivity was positive with 0.05% residual clonal plasma cells; response classified as complete response, MRD positive.
At age 69, started lenalidomide maintenance at 10 mg with plan for indefinite treatment given high-risk cytogenetics with t(4;14) and gain(1q21).

At age 70, one year post-transplant; maintained in complete response; immunofixation became negative indicating deepening to stringent complete response; repeat MRD at 10^-5 now negative; lenalidomide maintenance continued at 10 mg.
At age 70, developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 anemia with hemoglobin 10.8 g/dL on lenalidomide maintenance; dose reduced to 5 mg; fatigue improved.
At age 71, two years post-transplant; sustained stringent complete response with MRD negativity; peripheral neuropathy improved to grade 1; continued on lenalidomide 5 mg maintenance.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 68, bone marrow biopsy from site C42.1 showed 55% plasma cells with histology consistent with multiple myeloma (ICD-O morphology 9732/3); immunohistochemistry positive for CD138, CD38, and cytoplasmic IgG kappa; CD56 negative which is atypical; flow cytometry confirmed aberrant plasma cells comprising 48% of total cellularity.

GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

RESULTS:
At age 68, FISH analysis on bone marrow aspirate showed t(4;14) in 55% of plasma cells; no del(17p) or t(14;16); gain(1q21) detected in 30% of cells; R-ISS Stage III based on ISS III, LDH elevated at 265 U/L, and high-risk t(4;14).

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 68, serum protein electrophoresis showed M-spike of 5.1 g/dL in the gamma region; immunofixation confirmed IgG kappa monoclonal protein; serum free light chain kappa 310 mg/L, lambda 15 mg/L, kappa/lambda ratio 20.7; marked rouleaux formation on peripheral blood smear; serum viscosity mildly elevated at 2.8 centipoise.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

RADIOLOGY REPORT

FINDINGS:
At age 68, PET/CT showed widespread FDG-avid skeletal disease involving the axial and appendicular skeleton with at least 15 focal lesions; largest in the right 8th rib measuring 3.8 cm with cortical destruction; FDG-avid lesion in the sacrum with SUV 9.2; no extramedullary plasmacytomas.

Please correlate clinically.

FINDINGS:
At age 68, MRI of the pelvis and femora showed diffuse marrow replacement with multiple focal lesions in the sacrum and bilateral iliac bones; a 2.5 cm lesion in the left femoral neck raised concern for pathologic fracture risk and orthopedic consultation was recommended.

Please correlate clinically.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 68, initiated VRd induction therapy with standard dosing on 28-day cycles; plan for 4 to 6 cycles followed by consideration of autologous stem cell transplant; started acyclovir prophylaxis and low-molecular-weight heparin for DVT prevention given higher thrombotic risk.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 69, pre-transplant evaluation confirmed fitness for autologous stem cell transplant despite age 69; cardiac evaluation showed EF 58% with no significant valvular disease; pulmonary function adequate.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 69, day plus 100 bone marrow biopsy showed 4% plasma cells; SPEP no M-spike; immunofixation showed faint IgG kappa band; MRD by multiparameter flow cytometry at 10^-5 sensitivity was positive with 0.05% residual clonal plasma cells; response classified as complete response, MRD positive.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 70, one year post-transplant; maintained in complete response; immunofixation became negative indicating deepening to stringent complete response; repeat MRD at 10^-5 now negative; lenalidomide maintenance continued at 10 mg.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 71, two years post-transplant; sustained stringent complete response with MRD negativity; peripheral neuropathy improved to grade 1; continued on lenalidomide 5 mg maintenance.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Multiple myeloma, ISS stage II-III. IgG kappa or IgA lambda subtypes. Treated with VRd (bortezomib, lenalidomide, dexamethasone) induction followed by autologous stem cell transplant and lenalidomide maintenance. Include serum protein electrophoresis, free light chains, beta-2 microglobulin, FISH for del(17p) and t(4;14).
